rows.hypothesis: holders>=250 (backfill) is #1 on best-combos leaderboard (opt_avg_ret=24.25%, n=396, Panel7 ROBUST degradation=-0.38pp at TP150/SL30). Only combo at this return level with ROBUST walk-forward. Propose shadow strategy to collect real-time measured data (currently backfill-only). Target accumulating >=0.5 SOL with drop_top3>0 on n=100.

Copy entries on promotable/smart lead-wallet buys with static TP100/SL30. Hypothesis: lead-wallet selection (private info) carries edge that survives a 5% exit penalty, open-position MTM, and per-day regime splits, where all public T+30 chart features failed.

Pure copy: enter on lead buy, exit on lead sell (no own TP/SL). Tests whether the lead wallet's exit timing is itself the edge. Highest n of all copy variants; median hold ~105s keeps regime exposure small.

Copy only when >=2 tracked smart wallets buy the same token (consensus filter on top of copy signal). Lower n but cleaner signal; tests whether consensus concentrates the edge enough to beat single-wallet copying per trade.
holders>=250 filter ROBUST walk-forward (test=+20.02%, degradation=-0.54pp). Only holders threshold surviving Panel 7. Shadow test at TP150/SL30.
holders>=250 (backfill) opt_avg_ret=24.25% at TP150/SL30, n=396, Panel7 ROBUST (deg=-0.54pp). Best undeployed signal. Target >=0.5 SOL, drop_top3>0 at n=100.

buyers/trade >= 0.07 captures the smart-money 'broad organic demand, low churn' signature (the two strongest separators d=+0.96 & -0.79 combined into one scale-invariant ratio, smart 0.13 vs rest 0.04) and accumulates >= 0.5 SOL with drop_top3 > 0 on n>=100.

Full multivariate smart-money signature (buyer/trade + low whale + high buy_ratio + shape guard) with a let-it-run trailing exit (TP200, breakeven@15, trail 20% after +40%, hold 600s) probes whether the SM 'holds longer' behavior is capturable inside the T+600 backtestable window. Target >= 0.5 SOL, drop_top3 > 0, n>=100.

Low trade-count (churn) + broad buyer base + high buy_ratio is the smart-money signature (trade_count d=-0.79, the strongest LOW separator; buyers d=+0.96). This high-coverage T+30 proxy for smart-money presence should accumulate >= 0.5 SOL with drop_top3 > 0 on n>=100 where the rare real-time consensus signal (v53) couldn't reach n.

Counterintuitive: smart-money tokens have MORE snipers (count d=+0.51) and more EXPERIENCED snipers (wallet_velocity_max d=+0.43). Every momentum strategy excludes snipers; requiring >=1 experienced sniper + low whale concentration tests whether smart money follows skilled snipers. Target >= 0.5 SOL, drop_top3 > 0, n>=100.

Absolute buy-flow magnitude in the 0-30s window (flow_sol_buys d=+0.48) + broad buyer base + buy_ratio captures the SOL-weighted demand side of the smart-money signature, distinct from the count-based flow_imbalance already tested. Target >= 0.5 SOL, drop_top3 > 0, n>=100.

v9shadow-velmono-dev (vel20-50 + mono>0.5 + dev>5% @ 100/30) accumulates SOL but its edge is time-varying. Gating entries on its paired v9 source flag == STRENGTHENING (strength_only, signal=v9shadow-velmono-dev/shadow) should concentrate trading into the source's hot windows and lift net SOL / drop_top3 vs the always-on parent, at the cost of fewer trades.

v9shadow-velmid-liq-mono (vel20-50 + liq>100 + mono>0.33 @ 150/25) is a fat-tail strategy with negative lifetime median but positive recent edge. Gating entries on its paired v9 source flag == STRENGTHENING (strength_only, signal=v9shadow-velmid-liq-mono/shadow) should skip the cold windows that drag net SOL and improve drop_top3 robustness vs the always-on parent.

holders>=250 (backfill) sits #2 on leaderboard with opt_avg_ret=24.25%, n=396, panel7 ROBUST (0.54pp degradation). Propose shadow test to validate on live graduation stream. Target accumulating >=0.5 SOL with drop3>0 on n>=100.
gini<0.4 + climbing_t30_t45 is #7 on best-combos (opt_avg_ret=16.38%, n=462). Propose shadow test to validate on live data. Target accumulating >=0.5 SOL with drop3>0 on n>=100.

Headline smart-money finding: when >=3 money-edge wallets buy a graduation in the first 30s, it pumps 52% of the time (+12% avg return) vs 36% baseline (p~0). Latency-free T+30 filter. TP150 to let the consensus winners run.
Comparison tier: >=2 smart wallets in first 30s (44% pump rate). Tests whether 2 is enough or 3+ is the real threshold.

Smart money favors shallow early drawdown + smooth monotonic action (M2 Cohen's d 0.50 / 0.25). Pure T+30 shape filters (no late fields) — cleanest selection-replication test. Target >=0.5 SOL net, drop_top3>0 on n>=100.
Smart-bought tokens show broad genuine demand: more unique buyers (d=0.88), higher buy ratio (d=0.79), lower whale dominance (d=-0.58). Tests whether the organic-demand profile accumulates SOL.
Low whale dominance + high buy ratio + strong flow imbalance = the low-manipulation tokens smart money picks. Tighter SL20 since their drawdowns are shallow.
Smart money avoids serial-launcher creators (creator_prior_token_count 0.26 vs 3.16, d=-0.31). Fresh-dev + smooth shape, wider TP150 to let winners run.
Strictest smooth+demand profile with a truncated left tail (SL15 + breakeven@+30% + tighten-to-7% at half-hold). Tests whether shallow-drawdown selection makes a tight stop net-positive.

Direct selection copy: tokens a money-edge wallet bought in the first 30s (smart_money_early_count>=1). M3 showed +4.9pp PUMP rate (p=0.027) for this set. Forward-only/look-ahead-clean. Activates once filter-B code is deployed.

Two-checkpoint monotonic-up confirmation (pct_t15<t30<t45) + shallow early drawdown (maxDD_0_30>-10%) is a stricter, less-noisy version of v25's single climb check. Entering only on confirmed multi-point recovery should lift drop_top3 robustness vs v25 in a RED tape by rejecting fade-then-die candidates.

v25's exact entry but with the left tail truncated: SL10 (vs 30) + 1s poll + breakeven@+30% + tighten-to-7% at half-hold. Thesis from peak-analysis: universe EV is negative at every TP because non-hit trades bleed ~-31%; capping downside should make net SOL regime-stable even if win rate drops.

rows.strategy_label: v51 capitulation-bounce: down 10-60% @t30 + bounce t45>t30, fast scalp
rows.hypothesis: Mean-reversion scalp, orthogonal to the all-momentum book: buy tokens down 10-60% at T+30 that confirm a bounce (t30>t15 AND t45>t30), exit fast (TP15/SL12/120s). Low correlation to climbing strategies should stabilize aggregate P&L. Honest prior: dip_and_recover flag is negligible for T+300, so this ONLY works as a fast scalp, never hold — shadow forward-test will confirm or kill.

rows.strategy_label: v51 quality-momentum: vel20-50 + liq>100 + age>10m + shallow DD + mono
rows.hypothesis: Stack the strongest single signals from peak-analysis on the proven mid-velocity band: vel20-50 + liq>100 + age>10min (+4.1 avg final) + maxDD_0_30>-10% (+4.4, best path filter) + mono>0.5. Stricter cousin of v9shadow-velmid-liq-mono with the drawdown + age gates added.

gini < 0.4 + climbing_t30_t45 — low-concentration token filter not yet in shadow. Ranked #5 in best-combos (n=376, opt_avg_ret=+18.06% at TP150/SL30). With the climbing cluster decaying, testing whether gini<0.4 isolates a more durable climbing subset. Target: accumulate ≥0.5 SOL with drop_top3 > 0 on n=125.

holders>=250 (backfill, n=396) is #2 combo by opt_avg_ret=24.25% with panel7 walk-forward ROBUST (test_avg_ret=19.04%). Not yet in shadow test. Propose shadow deployment with standard TP100/SL30 to evaluate live accumulation.

v50 cohort - first LIVE deployment of the edge-decay strength gate. Two strategies, identical params except executionMode (shadow vs live_micro) so the live-training dashboard can show a clean live-vs-shadow drag measurement, the way v44 / v45 pairs were set up. Filters mirror v25-bot-excl-climbing (the family's ON-TRACK control, drop3 +3.33 SOL at n=520). Gate: edgeDecayGate=strength_only with signal=v25-bot-excl-climbing/shadow - only enters when the reference strategy is in STRENGTHENING state per /edge-decay (recent-30 median > lifetime median + 5pp). Differs from v48a in two ways: (1) 1s poll instead of 5s (catches fast SL/TP transitions, mirrors v32 / v44-1s-ttp10 mechanism), (2) 0.05 SOL trade size on both shadow and live for matched live-vs-shadow comparison. Coexists with v48a (5s poll, 0.5 SOL) as the smaller-step / faster-poll variant. Replaces the now-disabled v44-climb-1s-ttp10-live-micro as the family's single live strategy. Early v48a evidence: strength_only gate looked promising at small n. v50 tests whether that lift survives at the 0.05 SOL live size and whether 1s polling stacks an additional execution improvement on top of the strength gate. Caveat: the live drag is the unsolved problem - prior v44/v45 lives all flipped negative due to ~5-20pp avg execution cost per trade. The strength gate should help by reducing trade frequency to favorable windows only, but won't fix the per-trade execution cost itself.

Shadow control of the v50 live experiment. Same filters / TP=100 / SL=30 / entry@T+60 / 1s poll / 0.05 SOL trade size / edgeDecayGate=strength_only as v50-strength-live-micro. The only difference is executionMode=shadow vs live_micro - so any divergence between this and v50-strength-live-micro is pure live execution drag (slippage + tx land time + jito tip + fees), the same comparison logic v44 / v45 use in /live-training. Expected: per-trade mean and drop3 should track v48a-climb-strength-only (which is the same gate at 5s poll / 0.5 SOL); if it diverges, the 1s poll alone changes behavior. Volume should be ~30-50% of v48-climb-signal (the ungated baseline) since STRENGTHENING is the rarest of the three edge-decay states.

First live deployment of the strength_only edge-decay gate. Mirrors v50-strength-shadow exactly except executionMode=live_micro. The 0.05 SOL trade size matches the prior v44 / v45 live deployments so the per-trade execution cost (~5.85pp for v25-live, ~9.5pp for v44-climb-live, ~13.4pp for v44-1s-ttp10-live, ~20.2pp for v45-acc-gate-live) is directly comparable. Hypothesis: by only entering when the reference strategy is STRENGTHENING, we trade in higher-quality windows where the per-trade mean has room to absorb the live execution drag. If shadow runs at +5% mean and live drag is ~3pp, net live is +2% - profitable. Without the gate, the un-gated live versions have been running at -2 to -10% mean across recent weeks and bleeding through the sustained RED regime. Killed: v44-climb-1s-ttp10-live-micro (the only live strategy that remained from the v44 cohort) in the same command batch so v50-strength-live-micro becomes the sole active live experiment.

updates.note: Operator killed (disabled) the only live strategy 2026-06-10 — it was bleeding money in live_micro. Stops new live entries immediately; any open position closes on its own. Roster delete deferred pending operator call.

rows.hypothesis: gini < 0.4 + climbing_t30_t45 is the #4 combo in best-combos (n=356, opt_avg_ret=+19.08% at TP150/SL30). The gini < 0.4 filter selects very low token concentration — a new dimension not yet shadow-tested. Target accumulating >= 0.5 SOL with drop_top3 > 0 on n=100+.

Requiring continuation across BOTH T+15->30 and T+30->45 selects higher-conviction climbers. Fewer, better entries should lift net per-trade edge above the ~2.3pp live-execution cost wall that pins single-confirm v25 to ~0 SOL live.

Capping entry at +40% (vs +100%) drops already-extended climbers that carry the worst live slippage and mean-reversion. Removing those entries should raise realized net per-trade above the live cost wall while keeping the climbing edge.
Climbers fail in RED tape (live green-hour PnL ~flat, red clearly negative). Gating entries to non-RED regime should raise realized edge vs always-on v25 by skipping the worst windows.

v47 cohort - test dropping the dead-weight filters from v25-bot-excl-climbing. /counterfactual reports that 3 of v25's 5 filters are 'dead weight' (Δret pp at or near zero when removed): top5<=30% (+0.04pp), vel<200 (0pp), age>=60s (+0.16pp). Only dd>-25% (-1.26pp = pulls weight) and climbing_t30_t45 (-1.12pp) actually contribute to per-trade return. These dead-weight filters are flagged not because they're conceptually useless, but because they're too LOOSE to reject much: top5 rejects ~1% of rows, vel<200 rejects ~0%, age>=60s rejects ~3%. The cohort tests whether removing them improves trade coverage without hurting per-trade quality. Five variants: drop top5 only, drop vel only, drop age only, drop all three (lean = only dd + climbing), and lean + acc_t30>-8 (stacks the v40-acc-gate winning filter, which has been the only NEW filter to improve things in months). Success = drop3 >= v25-base AND n_trades > v25-base (more coverage, same edge). If the lean variant matches v25-base on drop3, the three dead-weight filters are confirmed redundant and can be dropped from the canonical bot-excl-climbing template.

Drop top5_wallet_pct<=30 from v25-base. Counterfactual: Δret +0.04pp when removed (dead weight), n with=1561, n w/o=1578 (+17 rows = ~1% more coverage). Expected: indistinguishable from v25-base on drop3 and per-trade quality, with marginal volume lift. If this variant deviates meaningfully from v25-base, the top5 filter is doing more than the counterfactual suggests.

Drop bc_velocity_sol_per_min<200 from v25-base. Counterfactual: Δret 0pp, n w/o = n with (0 rows added). The vel<200 filter rejects basically nothing in the current universe. Expected: this variant should be functionally IDENTICAL to v25-base on every metric. If it diverges, the filter was rejecting more than counterfactual measured.

Drop token_age_seconds>=60 from v25-base. Counterfactual: Δret +0.16pp (mildly improves on removal = dead weight), n w/o=1615 (+54 rows = +3% coverage). This filter was meant to skip very-young bonding-curve tokens that may have unstable T+30 metrics. If counterfactual is right, the climbing filter already implicitly excludes the same population.

The headline test: drop all three dead-weight filters at once. Only dd>-25% and climbing_t30_t45 remain. These are the two filters the counterfactual flagged as 'pulls weight' on v25-base. If the lean variant matches v25-base on drop3 (+5.17 SOL on n=477) with comparable or better coverage, the three dropped filters are confirmed redundant and can be removed from the canonical bot-excl-climbing template across all downstream cohorts. Expected volume lift: ~5-10% based on counterfactual coverage deltas (top5+1%, vel+0%, age+3%, with overlap).

Lean (dd + climbing) + acceleration_t30>-8. Combines two empirical findings: (1) the counterfactual dead-weight result motivating the lean base; (2) v40-acc-gate's positive result (drop3 +2.49, trim mean +5.14% at n=123) showing the acc_t30 filter pulls weight. Expected: should match or beat v40-acc-gate on per-trade quality AND match v47-lean on coverage. If this is the family's new winner, it becomes the canonical template - dd + climbing + acc.

Always-on ungated excl-climbing (v25 lineage) shadow strategy. Dual role: (1) generates the rolling edge-decay flag that gates the v48a/v48b executors, and (2) serves as the ungated control in the V48 A/B. If gating adds value, v48a/v48b should beat this baseline's net SOL and drop_top3.

rows.hypothesis: Trading excl-climbing ONLY while the v48-climb-signal baseline's edge-decay flag is STRENGTHENING (strict: STABLE/DECAYING/LOW-N all block) should accumulate net SOL with a better drop_top3 than the ungated baseline by sitting out cold streaks. Fewer trades, higher per-trade quality. Disabled until the edgeDecayGate code deploys; enable after deploy.

More trades than v48a, but should still beat the ungated baseline by avoiding the DECAYING regime. Disabled until the edgeDecayGate code deploys; enable after deploy.

Cumulative: 92 gate skips during periods when v25-base flag dipped to DECAYING earlier in the window; 10 trades during STABLE windows. Per the count math vs v48a (117 skips, 25 more than v48b), ~25 STABLE-period grads passed the gate but most then failed downstream (filter pipeline / capacity / momentum row), leaving 10 actual trades. Signal pointer retained at v25-bot-excl-climbing/shadow per the v48-climb-signal journal rationale.

rows.hypothesis: The bot-excl-climbing family's losses concentrate in slow-to-graduate tape: binning v44-climb-1s live hours by hourly median_ttg showed slow (>=750s) hours at -8.3 mSOL/trade vs fast (<550s) at +2.2 mSOL/trade. median_ttg = rolling median of token_age_seconds, so the per-token, leak-free analogue is an upper age bound. v47 mirrors the v25 base (best family performer: +7.48 SOL / n=477 / drop3 +5.17) and adds token_age_seconds<750. Predict the age cap lifts per-trade edge vs the ungated v25 control without cutting n so hard it can't clear the bar.

Same token_age<750 ttg-proxy gate as v47-base, on the 1s-poll variant (mirror of v32: +4.32 SOL / n=300 / drop3 +2.00). Tests whether the age cap stacks with tight SL-fill polling. Compare per-trade net vs ungated v32.

Same token_age<750 ttg-proxy gate as v47-base, on the 1s-poll + trailingTP=10% variant (mirror of v28: +5.26 SOL / n=340 / drop3 +1.89). Tests whether the age cap improves the best trailing-exit family member. Compare per-trade net vs ungated v28.

v46 cohort - first live test of the regime entry gate. Adds regimeGate to 5 shadow mirrors of proven base strategies (v25, acc-gate, v44-climb, v44-1s-ttp10). The regime classifier (universe-level pump_rate / fast_rug_rate over the last 50 grads) is computed independently by /regime-analysis and exposed to the trade-evaluator as a cheap cached snapshot. Prior 14-day overlay data showed RED hours net -0.0014 SOL/trade across 230 live trades, vs YELLOW +0.0014/trade across 90 trades, vs GREEN +0.0005/trade on 71 trades after threshold recalibration - a monotone-correct ordering. Goal: validate that gating live entries against this signal improves outlier-robust net SOL accumulation vs the un-gated control. Four variants use skip_red (block RED only, allow YELLOW + GREEN); one variant (v46-v25-green-only) uses green_only as the more restrictive A/B. Sample-size caveat: at ~60% RED time-in-regime, the gated strategies will see ~40% of the trade volume of the un-gated control. Need n>=80 to evaluate drop3 vs base.

Mainline regime-gate test. Mirror of v25-bot-excl-climbing (ON-TRACK, n=450, drop3 +5.94) with regimeGate=skip_red - skips entries when current regime is RED, allows GREEN + YELLOW. Hypothesis: cuts RED-hour entries (which run -0.0014 SOL/trade on the un-gated control) without sacrificing the YELLOW+GREEN winners. Expected volume: ~40% of v25 (since RED is ~60% of recent time). If per-trade mean rises meaningfully AND drop3 stays positive at n>=100, the gate is value-additive. Compare directly to v46-v25-green-only (the more restrictive A/B) to see whether YELLOW hours are worth keeping.

updates.note: 4h->14h checkpoint (2026-06-02 03:30Z): regime-gate thesis trending INVALID. The last 12h were the BEST tape of the experiment (7 GREEN / 5 YELLOW / 0 RED) yet every gated v46 strategy lost MORE, not less: v46-v25-skip-red n=28 total -1.74 drop3 -3.00; v44-1s-ttp10-skip-red -2.12; acc-gate-skip-red -2.15; v25-green-only -1.62. Over the SAME window the ungated controls profited: v25-bot-excl-climbing +6.55 (n=496), v28 +4.09, v32 +3.24. Same strategies, same tape, only diff = the regime gate -> the gate is subtractive, removing net-positive trades and leaving a worse residual set. drop_top3 more negative than total everywhere = broad losses, no carrying winners. Losing in the gate's best-case regime contradicts the core 'trade green, skip red' hypothesis. Kill criterion (n>=100 and net_sol<0) not formally tripped; holding to n~50 for a well-powered verdict, but lean is INVALID.

rows.hypothesis: Strongest expected lift in the v46 cohort. Mirror of v40-acc-gate (the standout new candidate, trim mean +6.92% at n=99, drop3 +2.79) with regimeGate=skip_red. acc-gate already showed the strongest pump_rate lag-correlation (+0.418 at 5h lead) - it is the strategy most sensitive to the universe's right-tail strength, so it should also be the one most helped by skipping the RED windows where the right tail is absent. Volume cost: probably 40-50% of acc-gate's un-gated volume.

updates.note: 4h->14h checkpoint (2026-06-02 03:30Z): n=20 total -2.15 drop3 -2.79 during GREEN-heavy overnight. Deepened from -0.97 at the 4h check. Ungated acc-gate family positive same window. Regime gate subtractive. Holding to n~50 then likely INVALID.

rows.hypothesis: Cross-base sanity check. v44-climb is the same filter set as v25-base but its live variant has been struggling with execution drag (live net -0.14 SOL at n=64). Shadow control of v44-climb at 0.5 SOL should track v25-base closely. Adding skip_red here tests whether the regime gate behaves consistently when the underlying base is the same as v46-v25-skip-red - they should produce near-identical numbers. If v46-v44climb-skip-red diverges meaningfully from v46-v25-skip-red, something else is going on (filter-evaluation race, signal staleness across instances, etc.).

updates.note: 4h->14h checkpoint (2026-06-02 03:30Z): n=28 total -1.74 drop3 -3.00 during a 7-GREEN/5-YELLOW/0-RED overnight. Ungated control v44-family + v25 profited over the same window. Regime gate appears subtractive. See v46-v25-skip-red note for the full cross-strategy comparison. Holding to n~50 then likely INVALID kill of the cohort.

rows.hypothesis: Stack two improvements: 1s poll + trailing TP 10% drop (matches v44-climb-1s-ttp10 - the only v44 live variant still net-positive at n=63) PLUS regimeGate=skip_red. Hypothesis: the 1s poll catches fast SL/TP transitions better while skip_red prunes the worst windows for those transitions to fire in. If both compound, this should beat both v46-v25-skip-red (no 1s poll) and v44-climb-1s-ttp10 (no regime gate) on drop3.

updates.note: 4h->14h checkpoint (2026-06-02 03:30Z): worst of the skip_red set, n=28 total -2.12 drop3 -3.16, in the best (GREEN-heavy) tape of the experiment. Ungated controls positive same window. Regime gate not separating this strategy's winners from losers. Holding to n~50 then likely INVALID.

rows.hypothesis: Most restrictive variant in v46. v25 base + regimeGate=green_only - only trades when current regime is GREEN (pump_rate >= 20% AND fast_rug_rate <= 25%). At ~18-19% time-in-regime for GREEN, this variant will see ~20% of v25's volume. Hypothesis: trades highest-quality windows only, expect highest mean% but lowest n. A vs B with v46-v25-skip-red (which also keeps YELLOW): if green_only beats skip_red, YELLOW hours aren't worth the trade. If skip_red beats green_only, YELLOW is still profitable enough to participate in. Low n caveat - may need 21+ days to hit n=60.

updates.note: 4h->14h checkpoint (2026-06-02 03:30Z): the STRICTEST gate (only trades GREEN) is among the worst per-trade: n=15 total -1.62 drop3 -1.93. If anything the tighter the regime gate, the worse the result -- the opposite of the hypothesis. Strongest single evidence the universe GREEN/RED label does not separate this strategy's good trades from bad. Candidate for earliest kill of the v46 cohort.

rows.hypothesis: v44 climbing + 1s poll + trailingTP10% live at 0.05 SOL: faster polling + trailing TP should capture more upside than Pair A; compare 1:1 vs shadow twin v44-climb-1s-ttp10-shadow.

v43 SL-defense cohort on the v25-bot-excl-climbing universe (top5<=30 + dd>-25 + vel<200 + age>=60 + climbing_t30_t45; TP100/SL30/entry@T+60), executionMode=shadow, all on pollIntervalSec=1 (the proven overshoot fix: v28-1s is the top monthly earner). This arm adds a profit-armed trailing SL (arm at +15%, trail 7% below HWM). Rationale: sl-precursor near-miss=0 means winners never approach the -30% stop, so the 33% SL rate is genuine pop-then-fade + straight dumpers. A trailing SL converts the pop-then-fade subset into profitable/shallow exits instead of letting them bleed to -30% (which realizes -43%). Tighter-static-SL is deliberately avoided: panel4 shows SL30 is EV-optimal and v27-sl20 has drop3 -1.29.

v43 cohort (executionMode=shadow), looser profit-armed trailing SL (arm +20%, trail 10%) on the v25-bot-excl-climbing base + 1s poll. A/B vs v43-trailsl-15-7-1s to find whether a higher arm threshold / wider trail keeps more winners running while still cutting the pop-then-fade tail that currently bleeds into the -30% hard SL (33% SL rate, -43% mean realized). trailingSlActivationPct is 0 across all 111 existing strategies, so this whole mechanism family is untested.

v43 cohort (executionMode=shadow), time-decayed SL tightening (after 50% of max-hold = 150s, raise the SL floor from -30% to -12%) on the v25-bot-excl-climbing base + 1s poll. Targets problem (b) for the dominant SL bucket: 68% of v25 SLs fire AFTER 60s as slow bleeders (~1-2%/5s), yet still realize -43%. Capping their depth at ~-12% should sharply reduce the dive-below-trigger magnitude for the slow-bleeder subset. Expect a modest rise in SL count (catches -12..-30 bleeders earlier) traded for much shallower losses; net SOL should improve if the bleeders rarely recover (near-miss=0 supports this).

v43 cohort (executionMode=shadow), profit-armed trailing SL (arm +20%, trail 10%) STACKED with a breakeven stop (floor to entry once HWM hits +15%) on the v25-bot-excl-climbing base + 1s poll. Two layered protections: breakeven removes downside on any trade that reaches +15%, trailing SL captures upside on rollovers. Both only RAISE the floor (per position-manager layering), so they never widen risk. Tests whether stacking beats either single overlay at cutting the 33% SL rate / -43% realized.

v43 cohort (executionMode=shadow), attacks problem (a) at the ENTRY: add monotonicity_0_30>0.5 (the single strongest dump/pump discriminator, Cohen's d 0.513 in price-path-stats; best EV among panel4 climbing-universe proxies at -10.4 / 41% WR) on top of the v25-bot-excl-climbing base, plus profit-armed trailing SL (20/10) + 1s poll. Since winners never approach the stop (near-miss=0), the only way to cut SL COUNT is to not enter the dumpers; monotonicity is the highest-signal exclusion not yet stacked here. Risk: mono>0.5 may be partly redundant with climbing_t30_t45 and could cut n hard.

v42 cohort - breakeven-stop DPM sweep on the v25-bot-excl-climbing universe. All 5 mirrors share v25 base (TP=100/SL=30, entry@T+60, 5s poll, no trailing TP, filters: top5<=30 + dd>-25 + vel<200 + age>=60 + climbing_t30_t45=1). Only breakevenStopPct varies across {10, 15, 20, 30, 40}. Mechanic (position-manager.ts ~line 481): once the position's high-water-mark reaches entry x (1 + breakevenStopPct/100), the SL floor is raised to the entry price. A token that pumps past the activation level then reverses exits at ~scratch (0% gross, slightly negative net after gap penalty + slippage costs) instead of the full -30% SL. This directly attacks the dominant loser pattern observed in loss-postmortem: trades that briefly pump then crash to a -30% SL fire (e.g. grad 23115 entered +70%, grad 23283 entered +35% - both touched positive territory before the rug). CAVEAT (note for future sessions): pure breakeven locks in NO profit, only protects capital - it converts would-be SL losses into scratches. The cost is false exits: trades that wiggle up to the activation level, dip back to entry (breakeven fires), then recover to TP - those become scratch exits instead of wins. Lower activation (10) = more protection + more false exits; higher activation (40) = less of both. Goal per the operator: lock in gains when a token at least moves up, by removing the give-it-all-back tail. Success = a breakeven level that lifts total_net_sol AND drop_top3 above the v25 control (+4.95 / +2.64 at n=352) by cutting the catastrophic SL tail faster than it sacrifices recoveries. If ALL levels underperform v25, the false-exit cost dominates and breakeven is net-negative on this universe (the v25-bot-excl-breakeven experiment at breakevenStopPct=20 previously went -0.41 SOL on n=86 - this cohort retests across a wider activation range with the now-larger v25 baseline).

Breakeven stop activates at +10% HWM. Most protective / earliest-engaging variant - the SL moves to entry as soon as the token is up 10%. Hypothesis: catches the largest share of pumped-then-dumped losers, converting many -30% SL fires into scratches. Risk: +10% is well inside the normal noise band (v25 p75 net is +26.7%), so a large fraction of eventual winners will wiggle to +10%, dip to entry, get stopped at breakeven, then run to TP without us - the highest false-exit rate of the cohort. Expected: lowest SL count, but also lowest TP count. Net effect is the open question - this is the variant most likely to over-protect.

Breakeven stop activates at +15% HWM. Slightly later engagement than the +10 variant - trades the marginal extra protection for fewer false exits. Tests whether the +10 to +15 step meaningfully reduces the false-exit rate while still catching most pump-then-dump losers. Expected to sit between v42-breakeven-10 and v42-breakeven-20 on both SL-reduction and false-exit cost.

Breakeven stop activates at +20% HWM. Direct re-test of the prior v25-bot-excl-breakeven experiment (breakevenStopPct=20, which went -0.41 SOL on n=86 and was deprioritized). Re-running on the now-larger/stronger v25 baseline (n=352, +4.95 SOL) to confirm or refute. +20% is just below the v25 p75 (+26.7%), so this activation only fires on trades that reach the upper quartile of the move distribution - should have a much lower false-exit rate than the +10/+15 variants while still protecting the bigger movers that reverse. If the prior -0.41 result holds, breakeven at this level is confirmed net-negative.

Breakeven stop activates at +30% HWM. Symmetric with the -30% SL distance - the token must move up as much as the SL allows it to move down before breakeven engages. Only fires on trades that reach +30%, which is past the p75 (+26.7%) - so it protects only the genuine movers (roughly the top ~20-25% of trades by peak). Lowest false-exit rate among the engaging variants; minimal interference with the normal noise band. Hypothesis: best balance - protects the give-it-all-back tail on real movers without prematurely stopping the noise-band winners. If any breakeven level beats v25, this or +40 is the most likely.

Breakeven stop activates at +40% HWM. Latest engagement / least protective variant - only the strong movers (40%+ peak, ~p80-p85 of the distribution) get the breakeven floor. Almost no false exits (a trade that hits +40% rarely round-trips to entry without us noticing), but also protects the fewest trades. Effectively a near-control: behaves like v25 on the bulk of trades, only differing on the subset that pumps 40%+ then fully reverses. Useful as the upper anchor of the activation sweep - if even +40 underperforms v25, breakeven adds no value at any level; if +40 ~ v25 but +10 is much worse, it confirms the false-exit cost rises sharply as activation drops.

v41 cohort - holder_count threshold sweep on the v25-bot-excl-climbing universe. All 5 mirrors share v25 base (TP=100/SL=30, entry@T+60, 5s poll, no trailing TP, filters: top5<=30 + dd>-25 + vel<200 + age>=60 + climbing_t30_t45=1). Each adds ONE holder_count filter at thresholds 15, 16, 17, 18, 19. KNOWN DATA QUALITY CAVEAT: holder_count is capped at 19 by getTokenLargestAccounts (step 1 in collector/holder-enrichment.ts:170) and the getProgramAccounts upgrade (step 2 ~line 254) is blocked by the current RPC provider, so 100% of the dataset has holder_count <= 19. Across 1,437 loser rows in loss-postmortem.json, the buckets are: <10 = 4.3%, 10-14 = 2.4%, 15-19 = 93.2%, >=20 = 0%. Therefore: (a) >=15 excludes only the bottom ~6.7% of trades (the genuinely thin-holder tokens), (b) >=19 keeps only tokens that HIT the cap (i.e., have at least 19 unique non-infrastructure holders in the top-20 RPC slice). The progressive sweep tests whether thin-holder tokens are systematically lower-quality entries, and where the threshold starts hurting volume more than it helps quality. Expected volume curve: >=15 nearly matches v25, dropping monotonically to >=19 which keeps ~93% of v25's universe.

Holder threshold sweep - lowest cutoff. Filters out only the bottom 6.7% of the universe (<15 holders). Hypothesis: tokens with <10 holders (4.3% of population) are the truly thin-LP rug-prone cases - excluding them removes high-variance tails without sacrificing meaningful coverage. Expected volume: ~93-95% of v25 baseline (largest sample size in the cohort). If this variant matches or barely beats v25, the very-thin-holder tail is small enough that filtering it doesn't move the needle on aggregate P&L. Best as the 'permissive control' for the cohort.

Holder threshold sweep - mid-low cutoff. Cuts the bottom ~7-8% of the universe (<16 holders). Tests whether the discrimination between holder_count of 15 vs 16 has any signal, given step 1 of holder enrichment progressively fills the top-20 RPC slice. Realistically expected to behave similarly to v41-holders-15 if the metric has noise in this band.

Holder threshold sweep - mid cutoff. Cuts roughly the bottom 8-9% (<17 holders). Mid-point of the sweep - finest-grained step in the dataset, where the curve should either remain flat (if 15/16/17 thresholds discriminate similar populations) or start to bend (if thin-but-not-tiny holder tokens have a distinct loss signature).

Holder threshold sweep - second-tightest. Cuts the bottom ~10-12% of the universe (<18 holders). Note: in the v25-bot-excl-climbing loss-postmortem, 2 of 20 worst losers had holder_count=18 (grad 23674 -90.2% and grad 23552 -65.2%). Filtering at >=18 would have kept those losers - so this threshold's discrimination is largely in the 'background' trade population, not at the loss tail. Useful comparison point against >=19.

Holder threshold sweep - the cap. Keeps ONLY tokens that hit the holder_count cap at 19 (~93% of v25's universe). This is effectively a binary 'did getTokenLargestAccounts return at least 19 non-infrastructure accounts' filter - functionally equivalent to 'token has enough holders to fill the top-20 RPC slice'. Since 18 of 20 v25 worst losers had holder_count=19, this threshold does NOT cut the loss tail by much. Expected: very close to v25 baseline performance. The value of this variant is as a comparison anchor for the lower thresholds - if 15/16/17 substantially beat 19, the small <19 tail is disproportionately loss-prone. If 19 beats them, the metric is just noise and tighter is better.

v40 cohort - defensive overlay sweep on the v25-bot-excl-climbing universe. All 5 mirrors share v25 base (TP=100/SL=30, entry@T+60, 5s poll, no trailing TP, filters: top5<=30 + dd>-25 + vel<200 + age>=60 + climbing_t30_t45=1). Each adds ONE defensive filter informed by loss-postmortem dominant_patterns: (1) acceleration_t30 > -8 cuts strongly-decelerating pre-entry tokens (bucket <-7.68 had 30% losers vs 20% baseline); (2) buy_pressure_whale_pct < 0.30 caps anchor-buyer dominance (live_micro postmortem flagged [0.35,0.51] at 46.7% losers); (3) top5_wallet_pct <= 20 tightens existing concentration filter; (4) vel in [5,100] excludes both grinder and frothy-fastrunner tails (55% losers had vel<5, 30% had vel>30); (5) acc + whale stacked compounds the 2 strongest signals. Goal: identify which defensive overlay improves drop_top3 robustness without crushing trade volume. Note: holder_count >= N was REJECTED as a filter candidate - getTokenLargestAccounts caps at 19 (step 1 in collector/holder-enrichment.ts), and the getProgramAccounts upgrade (step 2) is blocked by current RPC provider - so 100% of dataset has holder_count <= 19.

Defensive overlay: add acceleration_t30 > -8 on top of v25-bot-excl-climbing. Loss-postmortem dominant_patterns flagged bucket [-7.68, -1.88] as having 0% losers vs 20% baseline (-20pp deviation = strongest single-feature defense), while the strongly-decelerating bucket (acc < -7.68) had 6 of 20 losers (30% vs 20% baseline). Hypothesis: tokens whose price was decelerating sharply pre-entry tend to keep rolling over even when climbing_t30_t45=1 says recovery. Expected: cuts ~30% of fast-SL losers (grads 22642 acc=-12.66, 22569 acc=-12.71, 23552 acc=-10.84 would have been excluded). Volume should drop to ~80-85% of v25; per-trade mean should rise.

Defensive overlay: add buy_pressure_whale_pct < 0.30 on top of v25. The live-micro postmortem flagged the [0.35, 0.51] bucket as 46.7% losers vs 20.3% baseline (+26.4pp deviation = strongest signal in that cohort). Hypothesis: when ONE buyer is 35%+ of pre-T+30 SOL inflow, that anchor buyer is statistically likely to be the dumper that triggers the cascade. Excluding tokens where the largest buyer dominates removes that single-point-of-failure. Note: field is on 0-1 scale (verified vs queries.ts:925-926). Expected volume: 70-80% of v25 (whale concentration >30% is common in this universe).

Defensive overlay: tighten v25's top5_wallet_pct from <=30 to <=20. A/B test of whether the wider 30 ceiling is letting in too-concentrated tokens. Note: the loss-postmortem data is mixed - 12 of 20 v25 losers had top5<15% (low concentration) while only 5 had top5 in [20, 30]. So tightening to 20 cuts ~25% of losers but ALSO cuts a comparable slice of winners. This is the variant most likely to be NEUTRAL or NEGATIVE - included as an A/B control. If this beats v25, concentration is more predictive than the postmortem suggests. If it loses, concentration isn't the lever.

Defensive overlay: replace v25's vel<200 with vel in [5, 100]. Loss-postmortem shows v25 losers are bimodal on velocity - 55% have vel<5 (slow grinders, the climbing filter passed them on a small recovery but they roll over) and 30% have vel>30 (frothy fastrunners - high turnover, prone to fast reversal). The middle band vel [5,100] should keep most winners while cutting both tails. Note: this differs from v25-bot-excl-vel2050 (HIT-KILL at n=45) by using a WIDER band [5,100] not the narrow [20,50] - preserving more coverage.

Defensive overlay: stack the two strongest postmortem signals - acceleration_t30 > -8 AND buy_pressure_whale_pct < 0.30. Each addresses a different failure mode: acc filter cuts the 'already-rolling-over' tokens, whale filter cuts the 'one big buyer who will dump' tokens. If their loss populations are largely DISJOINT, stacking should compound the defense. If they overlap heavily (same tokens flagged by both), the second filter adds little. Expected volume: 60-70% of v25 - the most restrictive of the cohort.

v39 cohort - entry-gate sweep on the bot-excl-climbing universe. All 5 mirrors share v25-bot-excl-climbing params (filters: top5<=30 + dd>-25 + vel<200 + age>=60 + climbing_t30_t45=1; TP=100/SL=30/entry@T+60/5s poll/no trailing TP). Only entryGateMinPctT30 + entryGateMaxPctT30 vary. Outlier analysis of v25 showed 4 of 7 top losers had entry_pct_from_open > +35% (high-entry fast-rugs at SL in <60s), and big winners spanned the full entry range (+6% to +90%). The climbing_t30_t45 filter passes some dip-recoveries (price down at T+30 but rallying T+45) that the current min=+5 gate excludes - those may be the highest-asymmetry trades. Variants: (5,50) caps upper only; (-25,100) and (-50,100) extend lower; (-25,50) and (-50,50) combine. Goal: identify whether the v25 entry window can be reshaped to cut fast-SL losers without losing the right tail.

Upper-cap only test. Hypothesis: trimming entries with pct_t30 > +50% removes the late-entry fast-rug population (grad 23115 entered at +70% and died in 16s; grad 23889 entered at +63% and died in 109s) without sacrificing most v25 winners (only 1 of 7 top winners entered >+50%, namely grad 23060 at +89.98%). Expected: lower trade volume (~50% of v25), higher per-trade mean, lower SL rate. If trim_mean rises above v25's +0.74% with drop_top3 still positive, the cap is value-additive.

Allow modest dippers. Hypothesis: tokens that dipped to -25% by T+30 but are climbing by T+45 are dip-recovery plays - high asymmetric upside (lots of upside room) if the recovery sticks. The current v25 min=+5 hard-excludes these. Trade-off: dippers that DON'T fully recover hit SL fast. If climbing_t30_t45 is a strong-enough recovery signal, the asymmetric payoff should net positive. Expected volume: 110-130% of v25 (adds a population).

Aggressive dip-buyer. Hypothesis: -50% at T+30 with confirmed climb at T+45 is a 'caught knife' - either a true recovery (big upside, room to run multi-x) or a dead-cat bounce. Memecoin distribution should give a small percentage of these true recoveries that pay 200%+, offsetting the SL fires. Higher-variance bet vs (-25,100). If drop_top3 stays positive this is the highest-EV variant. If drop_top3 is negative, this is pure lottery-ticket noise.

Tightened both sides. Hypothesis: pct_t30 in [-25, +50] is the 'sweet spot' band - excludes both the late-momentum-chasers (>+50 entries) and the deep-dippers (<-25). Should produce the highest per-trade mean and the cleanest distribution shape. Cost: lowest trade volume of the 5 variants. If this wins on trim_mean + drop_top3 but loses on total_net_sol, the band is right but coverage is too narrow.

Wide-lower + capped-upper combo. Hypothesis: combine the asymmetric-upside of dip-recoveries (lower bound -50) with the fast-SL protection of capping high entries (upper bound 50). If both effects compound, this is the best variant. If they cancel (dippers add SL count faster than the cap saves), result will be neutral. Volume should be ~80-100% of v25 (gains dippers, loses high-entries).

buy_ratio>0.6 + confirmed_recovery has n=100 in best-combos with opt_avg_ret=+8.36% at TP75/SL30. Two independent signals: sustained buy pressure + price recovery. Should accumulate >=0.5 SOL with drop_top3>0 on n=125.

Auto-pair scanner (Panel 6) headline single-feature finding: firstbuyer_priors >= 3 has X-marginal mean PnL = +0.254 SOL on n=212 historical traded tokens. The first non-bot buyer in T+0..T+5s having sniped 3+ prior graduations signals quality. Only 1 existing strategy uses this column — clean. This strategy isolates the bare signal to confirm it stands alone before adding interaction filters (see v38-firstbuyer-flush for the rule-#1 combo).

Auto-pair scanner (Panel 6) RULE #1: firstbuyer_priors >= 3 AND max_tick_drop_0_30 < -7. Historical: mean PnL = +2.04 SOL on n=54 traded matches, lift = +1.78 over single-feature marginals (X alone +0.25, Y alone -0.05). Both features clean (1 + 0 existing strategies using them). The pattern: experienced first-buyer (quality signal) AND early flush (>7%+ single 5s drop) = asymmetric upside. Buy-the-dip-on-smart-money-interest thesis. WR likely 30-40% but mean PnL big due to fat-tail wins. Lower fill rate than v38-firstbuyer-pure (combined ~3% of grads vs ~10%) — kill criterion tightened to n>=30.

Auto-pair scanner (Panel 6) non-firstbuyer thesis: holder_count < 19 AND total_sol_raised >= 85. Historical mean PnL = +0.78 SOL on n=70 traded matches, lift = +0.73 over either marginal (holders alone +0.05, raised alone -0.11). Both clean (1 strategy uses holder_count, 0 use total_sol_raised). Captures 'thin token (few holders) but big SOL raise' — concentrated whale buys before graduation, conviction rather than dispersion. Diversifies the v38 cohort away from firstbuyer_priors dependence so we can isolate signal sources. Both filters work on T+0 context (no entry delay needed).

Loosened version of v36-conviction-buyers (which fired 0 trades in 36h). Cohort B Panel 2: whale_pct d=+0.224, unique_buyers d=-0.280. Untraded panel: both clean (|d|<0.15). Only 2 existing strategies reference these fields. Captures 'few large conviction buyers' pattern — real demand concentrated in whales, not coordinated sniper bots. Thresholds set from cohort B medians: whale_pct >= 0.25 (winner median 0.296, loser 0.236) and unique_buyers <= 15 (winner median 12, loser 18, loosened from 5 for fill rate). TP/SL=50/30 from best-combos empirical consensus.

Creator reputation filter on prior rug rate. Cohort B Panel 2: d=-0.201, with striking quartile gap — Bot-Q WR (creators with lowest rug rates) = 96.3% winner vs Top-Q WR (highest rug rates) = 55.6%. Panel 5: untraded d=-0.105 (very clean — no existing roster bias). Only 1 existing strategy uses this column. Threshold: prior_rug_rate < 0.30 (winner median 0.25, loser 0.37). Sample sizes smaller than other v37 candidates (winner_n=67, loser_n=42) but n>30 per side so not low-conf. TP/SL=50/30.

Pure flow-sells filter — zero existing strategies use flow_sol_sells_0_30, so this is a truly novel signal. Cohort B Panel 2: d=-0.223 (lower sells = winner). Quartile gap: Bot-Q WR (lowest sells) = 62.4% winner vs Top-Q WR (highest sells) = 40.9%. Panel 5: untraded d very low (<0.10 — completely clean). Threshold: flow_sol_sells_0_30 < 5 SOL (winner median 3.95, loser 6.06; slightly above winner median to balance signal vs fill rate). Captures 'tokens where dumpers aren't piling on in the first 30s' which is intuitively the early-rug filter the bot doesn't currently have. TP/SL=50/30.

price-path-v2 cohort B headline finding: tokens with ≤3 snipers in T+0..T+2 had ~100% top-quartile winner rate. Isolating as standalone filter at the wider ≤3 threshold should net ≥1.5 SOL on n≥100 with drop_top3>0. TP/SL=50/30 from best-combos empirical consensus (SL=30 picked in 10/12 high-n combos).

rows.hypothesis: price-path-v2 found a 2D pattern: high whale share of buy SOL (d=+0.25) AND few unique buyers (d=-0.29) both correlate with realized PnL. Translates to 'a few large conviction buyers, not many small bundler bots.' Novel — no current strategy captures this. Coverage gap: buy_pressure_* is new-only, so only fires on recent grads.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-26: 0 trades over 3+ days (fill rate 0.0/day). Original thresholds (whale_pct >= 50 AND buyers <= 5) were too tight to ever match. The successor v37-whale-conviction loosened thresholds to (whale_pct >= 0.25 AND buyers <= 15) and is now firing successfully (n=28, +0.295 SOL). rows.hypothesis: price-path-v2 cohort A's strongest non-tautological signals: monotonicity_0_30 d=+1.54 (87.7% top-Q WR) and max_drawdown_0_30 d=+0.36. Peak-analysis: mono>0.66 has p75_peak=54.2, dd>-10% has p75=53.3 and the best avg_final (+4.8%) of any single filter — TP=50 captures the p75 winners cleanly.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-26: hit auto-kill (n=92, total_net_sol=-1.088, drop_top3=-2.267). Kill criterion 'n>=60 and net_sol<-1' satisfied. Was profitable briefly (n=39, +0.49 SOL) but fully reverted as predicted by lottery-ticket warning. LESSON: monotonicity_0_30 was the canonical peak-prediction artifact. Cohort A d=+1.53 but Cohort B d=+0.12 (A↔B ratio 0.08x — 92% signal collapse). Also 17 existing strategies already filter on monotonicity — pure tautological discovery. The cross_cohort_ratio + StratUse columns added in the v2 dashboard would have rejected this candidate at design time.

rows.hypothesis: price-path-v2 cross-cohort robust signals: top10_wallet_pct negative d in BOTH cohorts (-0.25 / -0.26), flow_imbalance_t30 positive in BOTH (+0.48 / +0.24). Mirrors best-combo 'top10 < 25% + confirmed_recovery' (TP=50/SL=30, +14.22% on n=38) but uses the flow signal instead of confirmed_recovery — should fire more often.

Revised from initial 'small init_lp wins' v2 finding after cross-checking best-combos.json. init_lp > 15 is one of the top empirical performers (TP=50/SL=30, +10.42% on n=82 with confirmed_recovery). The v2 negative-d signal was at the EXTREME-low end (true rugs); the meaningful signal is init_lp >= 15. Pairing with flow_imbalance to avoid catching dying markets.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-26: hit auto-kill (n=60, total_net_sol=-1.811, drop_top3=-3.225). Kill criterion 'n>=50 and net_sol<-1' satisfied. LESSON: the 'init_lp >= 15' thesis was a misread of best-combos.json. The cited combo (init_lp > 15 + confirmed_recovery, +10.42%) drew its edge from confirmed_recovery, not init_lp. init_lp alone or paired with flow_imbalance has no forward edge. Going forward, when borrowing thresholds from best-combos.json, isolate the actual edge driver before promoting to a new strategy.

rows.hypothesis: Triple-stack of the three cross-cohort-confirmed price-path-v2 signals (sniper count negative, top10 wallet negative, flow imbalance positive). Wider thresholds than single-filter strategies (≤5 / <30 / >0) because AND-stacking already cuts volume. TP=75 instead of 50 because triple-quality entries justify a wider target. SL=30 stays — same empirical consensus.

Direct A/B vs the winning v9shadow-velmono-dev (vel 20-50 + mono>0.5 + dev>5%, +4.25 SOL on n=41). Tests whether LOW dev concentration outperforms HIGH dev in the same vel band. Prior dev<3% strategies all paired with slow velocity (vel<5) and never cleared 1 SOL. If dev<3% > dev>5% here, the high-dev result is a coincidence; if dev<3% < dev>5%, dev concentration is genuinely a positive signal in mid-vel - counter to conventional wisdom. Same TP/SL grid as parent (100/30) for clean apples-to-apples.

top10_wallet_pct has ZERO usage in the current roster despite being defined in FILTER_CATALOG. Tests the operator's 'no whale' hypothesis: filtering out tokens where the top-10 holders aggregate is >=40% should reduce catastrophic SL fires by removing whale-dump risk. Loose enough to preserve sample volume vs top5<10% (more permissive - wider entry). Pair with the proven vel 20-50 base; same grid as v10-best-double (TP150/SL25).

Strict-diversity variant of the 'no whale' thesis. top10<25% caps the top-10 aggregate share; wallet_gini_top20<0.6 enforces an even distribution among the top 20 (vs a few dominant holders). Tests whether DIVERSIFIED holder distribution is a positive entry signal in the vel 20-50 band. Expected to be sample-starved (two strict filters) but if it works the per-trade edge should be strong.

holder_count is essentially unused in the roster - only v11-vel100-holders-lotto uses it, and that's a high-velocity lottery ticket. Tests whether holder breadth at T+30 is a positive signal in mid-vel. holders>=10 should screen out tokens with concentrated early ownership (a single-whale signal) without being so restrictive as to drop sample volume.

Buy-pressure dimension is collected at T+35 (after the 30s entry delay) but currently used by ZERO strategies. Tests whether sustained buy-side activity in the entry window is a positive signal. buyers>=10 = broad participation (not a coordinated bundle); buy_ratio>0.6 = SOL flow majority buys, not sells. Stacks with vel 20-50; same grid as v10-best-double.

LP-deepening dimension: pumpswap_lp_growth_t0_to_t30_pct measures how much the PumpSwap pool grew from graduation to T+30. ZERO strategies use it. lp_growth>25% means the pool grew 25%+ post-graduation, which suggests active inflow rather than the typical post-grad LP drain. Tests whether liquidity-flow direction predicts trajectory.

We test batch DENSITY (busy_batch/quiet_batch) but never batch RANK. first_in_batch = this token was the first to graduate in a 5-min window. Hypothesis: first-mover gets the attention of the post-graduation flow; later ranks in the same batch suffer from attention dilution. Tests the rank dimension cleanly against mid-vel.

Triple-stack of the three known positive signals: vel 20-50 (top band), mono>0.5 (top single-filter winner #1, used in +4.25 SOL strategy), climbing_t30_t45 (recovery confirmation, key ingredient in the promotable v25-bot-excl-climbing). Each works individually; never tested all three together. Expect a strong per-trade edge but possibly thin sample volume. TP100/SL30 to match the climbing-style grid that v25 used.

v32 cohort — Experiment A: 1s polling ALONE (no trailing TP). 5 mirrors of top base performers (velmono-dev, best-double, vel20-top5, bot-excl-climbing, vel2050-dd-base). Isolates the pure 1s polling effect from the trailing TP confound that hurt v26. Expectation: lower SL realized losses (close to configured -SL% instead of -SL%×1.4 with gap penalty), unchanged TP capture rate, marginal net SOL improvement. If v32 beats base by ≥0.3 SOL on matched n, 1s polling has standalone value worth rolling out broadly. If null effect, the 5s polling cadence is fine for SL/TP and we can save the RPC budget.

v33 cohort — Experiment B: trailing TP with 5% drop (vs v26's 10%). 5 mirrors on the same universes. Hypothesis: the near-miss failure mode in v26 was driven by trailing TP exiting at peak-10% which lands below static TP. A tighter 5% drop captures the peak closer to its actual high, minimizing the near-miss loss while still allowing real moonshots to extend. Risk: tighter trail exits too early on real runners (gives up upside). vel20-top5 is the canary — already showed positive in v26 at 10% drop, see if 5% is even better.

v34 cohort — Experiment C: trailing TP with 10% drop AND minPeakLiftPct=20. Requires the trailingTpMinPeakLiftPct code change (PR claude/trailing-tp-min-peak-lift, commit 752ea74). Behavior: trailing only activates if post-entry peak clears TP × 1.20 (for TP=100, peak ≥ +120%; for TP=150, peak ≥ +180%). Near-miss trades that touch TP but don't clear min-lift exit at static TP fallback when price retraces below TP. Direct fix for the v26 near-miss failure mode. Hypothesis: this combines the best of both — static TP cleanup on near-misses + trailing capture on real moonshots. Expected to beat both base (static TP) and v26 (pure trail) on the same universes.

PROMOTED 2026-05-21 to live_micro (0.05 SOL/trade via MICRO_TRADE_SIZE_SOL override) after shadow v25-bot-excl-climbing cleared all 4 SOL-bar gates (composite score 100): n=188, total +2.86 SOL, drop_top3 +1.08 SOL, monthly_drop3 +4.64 SOL/mo. Regime stability: std-dev 13.72pp across the 12-bin sparkline — below the 15pp promotion bar. Auto-flag: STRENGTHENING (recent_30_median_pct +1.58, last_25 mean +3.52% / median +2.42%). Walk-forward (manual reconstruction since Panel 7 doesn't cover this custom combo): both halves negative mean-per-bin BUT total_net_sol positive due to fat-tail wins — classic memecoin profile, not OVERFIT. Conditions for live: start at micro (0.05 SOL), monitor first 50 live trades for drop3 stability, killswitch armed at DAILY_MAX_LOSS_SOL=1.0. Strategy: same filters as shadow control (top5<=30 + dd>-25 + vel<200 + age>=60s + climbing_t30_t45=1), same TP=100/SL=30/entry@T+60. Shadow v25-bot-excl-climbing remains enabled in parallel as the control.

Restoring the validated shadow filter set.

rows.hypothesis: v9shadow-vel20-top5 base + SOL filter that skips the worst 40% SOL days (Q1+Q2 = -93 SOL aggregate). Adversarial test: base is SOL-BEAR (spread -0.87, did better when SOL was DOWN) so we expect the filter to HURT this strategy. If it actually helps, the filter is doing more than regime alignment. Threshold derived from 60-day SOL quintile cutoffs (in-sample).

v9shadow-vel20-top5 base + BTC filter that skips Q1+Q2+Q5 tails (the three worst BTC quintiles). Adversarial test: base is BTC-BEAR (spread -0.79, did better when BTC was DOWN), so removing BTC-down days should HURT this strategy. Tests whether the BTC tail-skipping is enough overhead to overcome the directional misalignment.

v9shadow-vel20-top5 base + F&G filter (skip Extreme Fear days only). Extreme Fear avg -8.43%/trade vs Fear -3.08% - the deadliest regime by ~5pp. Keeps 86% of trading days. Base has insufficient Neutral data (n=5) to assess F&G sensitivity directly. If V31.3 beats baseline, the F&G filter is general-purpose; if neutral, the signal is only present in F&G-sensitive bases.

v10-best-double base + SOL filter. Aligned test: base is SOL-BULL (spread +1.72, Q5 net +1.00), so skipping bottom 40% SOL days should boost EV by removing -93 SOL aggregate worst regime. Expected to HELP. Paired with V31.1 (same filter on SOL-BEAR base) to isolate whether the filter is regime-amplifier (helps aligned, hurts opposite) or universal-positive.

v10-best-double base + BTC filter that skips Q1+Q2+Q5 tails. Base is BTC-BULL (spread +1.67, Q5 net +1.39) but the filter cuts the BTC>3.12% extreme tail too — a partial conflict with base preference. Tests whether tail-skipping outweighs upside-clipping. Expected HELP but smaller than V31.4 because the filter removes some of v10's best regime (Q5 BTC).

v10-best-double base + F&G filter (skip Extreme Fear). Base has no measurable F&G sensitivity. If V31.6 beats baseline by a similar margin to V31.3 (same filter on v9 base), the F&G filter is universal. If it doesn't budge, the F&G filter only helps F&G-sensitive bases. Expected ~2.8 trades/day.

Deployed 2026-05-20 after journal proposals from 05-19 and 05-20 sat in NO-DATA limbo (no commands.json upsert was sent). The combo `vel 50-200 + confirmed_recovery` is currently the #1 best-combo by opt_avg_ret (24.56% at n=40, opt cell TP=75/SL=30). Hypothesis: the high-velocity band (50-200 SOL/min — fast pumpers that aren't quite extreme bots) intersected with the C5 confirmed_recovery filter (dip_and_recover_flag=1 AND pct_t30>pct_t15 AND pct_t45>pct_t30) selects for tokens that pumped hard pre-graduation, briefly dipped post-grad, and recovered by T+45. The recovery signal acts as the path-confirmation overlay on a universe that otherwise has high variance. Risk factors: vel 50-200 is ~9% of population AND confirmed_recovery requires C5 path data — intersection coverage is sparse (~1-2% of grads), so target_n=100 in 21 days is tight. Deployed with v28 cohort conventions: pollIntervalSec=1 (1s SL/TP timing) + trailingTpEnabled=true / dropPct=10. entryTimingSec=60 to guarantee recovery_t45_above_t30 is written before evaluation.
vel 50-200 + confirmed_recovery is #1 best-combo by opt_avg_ret (24.56%, n=40, TP=75/SL=30). Journal proposal exists from 05-19 with NO-DATA status — no strategy-commands.json push has been sent yet.

Restricting v10-best-double to days where BTC daily return <= +0.08% (skipping the BTC-up tercile). trends-market.json shows aggregate -26.86 SOL on BTC-up days (Q3) vs +10.57 SOL on BTC-flat days (Q2). Filter removes the bot's worst regime while keeping ~67% of trades. Expected ~2.3 trades/day vs 3.5 base.

Same BTC filter as V30.1 applied to a different base (v9-vel20-top5: low-velocity + low-concentration). Cross-base test — if both V30.1 and V30.2 improve over their unfiltered bases, the BTC-up effect generalizes. If only one improves, it's strategy-specific noise. Expected ~1.5 trades/day vs 2.2 base.

Stacks BTC filter (<=+0.08%) and F&G filter (>=25, skip Extreme Fear only). Same base as V30.1. Tests whether filters compound additively or one dominates. If V30.3 outperforms V30.1, F&G adds value on top of BTC. If equal, BTC filter alone captures the signal. Expected ~2.1 trades/day vs 3.5 base.

Restricting v10-best-double to UTC 02-10 (the +17pp best-hour band per trends-time.json aggregate) reduces SL frequency and lifts per-trade EV vs the unrestricted base. Caveat: v10-best-double is a SOL-BULL strategy (does better when SOL is up) per trends-market.json, so result will be muddled by SOL/BTC regime noise.

Restricting v9shadow-velmono-dev (#1 by drop_top3 +2.42 SOL, monthly +6.84 SOL) to UTC 02-10. Base has best outlier robustness in roster, so the gate is reducing tail-risk exposure during the bad afternoon band (UTC 14-21).

Restricting v9shadow-vel20-top5 (n=59, monthly +5.18, distinct filter: low velocity + low top5) to UTC 02-10. Higher-volume v9 leg of V29 cohort; tests whether the hour gate is universal-positive across v9 filter compositions or specific to dev/mono combos.

Mirror of v9shadow-velmono-dev (currently #1 by net SOL: +4.08 SOL, 21.46% avg, 11 TP / 14 SL / 13 TO on n=38) with TWO changes: pollIntervalSec=1 (1s poll instead of 5s) + trailingTpEnabled=true / dropPct=10. Hypothesis: 1s polling catches fast SL/TP transitions earlier — on a -50%-in-2s rug, current 5s polling fires SL at -50% (the price at the next poll), while 1s polling fires closer to the configured -30%. Estimated savings: ~10pp per SL fire on the fast-rug subset. Trailing TP=10% adds the right-tail capture on top.

Mirror of v10-best-double (#2 by net SOL: +3.38 SOL, 6.83% avg, 5 TP / 48 SL / 46 TO on n=99). The SL count (48) is the dominant exit reason — this is THE strategy that should benefit most from 1s polling if the fast-rug hypothesis holds, because most of those 48 SL fires likely realized worse than -25% net due to the 5s polling gap. Trailing TP=10% added on the few TPs that do fire (5 currently). Combined effect should lift the avg per-trade by 2-4pp.

Mirror of v9shadow-vel20-top5 (#3 by net SOL: +3.14 SOL, 10.65% avg, 5 TP / 13 SL / 41 TO on n=59) with 1s poll + trailingTP=10%. Notable: 41 of 59 exits are timeouts — this strategy reaches T+300 with the position still open in 69% of cases. The peak-analysis suggests these timeouts happen near the peak with a slow bleed. 1s polling on TP detection might convert some timeouts to clean TP exits if a brief +150% touch happens between current 5s poll ticks.

Mirror of v9shadow-velmid-liq-mono (#4 by net SOL: +2.84 SOL, 13.22% avg, 3 TP / 21 SL / 19 TO on n=43). Only strategy in the v28 cohort not already mirrored in v26. The vel20-50+liq>100+mono>0.33 combo has the highest avg-net-return per trade of the top 4 but the lowest n — adding 1s poll + trailingTP=10% should both reduce the SL pain (21 fires) and capture more of the right-tail.

Mirror of v25-bot-excl-climbing (#5 by net SOL: +2.10 SOL, 2.96% avg, 16 TP / 49 SL / 77 TO on n=142 — also TOP by monthly run rate at +36+ SOL/mo). 49 SL fires is the highest absolute SL count in the v28 cohort — if the 1s-poll fast-rug hypothesis is real anywhere, it should show here. The C5 climbing entry filter already pre-selects for confirmed-rising paths so the surviving SL fires are presumably fast unexpected reversals — exactly the case 1s polling helps with.

Mirror of v20-vel2050-dd-base (#6 by net SOL: +2.07 SOL, 9% avg, 4 TP / 22 SL / 22 TO on n=46). Same filter universe as v10-best-double but with entry-gate [5,100] instead of [-99,1000] — cleaner comparison of the 1s-poll effect since the entry-gated population has a more focused distribution.
vel 50-200 + confirmed_recovery is #1 best-combo by opt_avg_ret (22.54%, n=38, TP=75/SL=30). No shadow strategy currently tests this velocity band x recovery combination.

v21-bottom-excluded variant: TP=100→75, SL=30 unchanged. Direct test of the peak-analysis.json suggested_tp finding — TP=75 has hit rate 9.6% with expected_return_pct +3.11 (the local maximum), vs TP=100 at hit rate 5.4% / EV +2.73. The base strategy's top winners (from strategy-percentiles paper sample) all peaked at +30-45% then bled to timeout — TP=75 would have captured those as TPs instead. Control: v21-bottom-excluded (TP=100/SL=30, n=243, net +2.5 SOL, +6% avg). If TP=75 beats by ≥0.5 SOL at matched n, peak-analysis-EV is the better predictor than sim counterfactual for this universe.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-20 — INVALID at n=100+: total<0, drop_top3<0. HIT-KILL. The peak-analysis-EV hypothesis (TP=75 as the EV maximum vs TP=100) was falsified. Peak-analysis suggested +3.11% EV at TP=75 vs +2.73% at TP=100 — but live data on n=100+ trades shows TP=75 loses money on the bot-excluded universe. Why peak-analysis misled: max_relret_0_300 is a LOOK-AHEAD signal computed across the full T+0..T+300 window — it counts a TP-eligible peak even if the price spent most of the window dumping. The realized exit (with gap penalty + slippage) on a brief +75% touch in an otherwise-dumping window is meaningfully worse than the look-ahead suggests. Lesson: peak-analysis CDFs are DIAGNOSTIC ONLY. Trust the sim counterfactual (which actually simulates the exit) over peak-analysis EV when they disagree.

rows.hypothesis: v21-bottom-excluded variant: TP=100→75, SL=30→20. Stacks two changes — peak-EV TP plus tighter SL to reduce gap-penalty pain (current SL=30 with 20% gap penalty = -36% net realized per SL fire; SL=20 with same gap = -24% realized, saving ~12pp per fire). Tradeoff: more SL fires from trades that briefly dip but would have recovered. Net effect depends on how many of the current ~34% SL rate trades actually recovered to TP — if most just bled out, this is pure win. If many would have run, this trims winners. Estimated lift: 1.5-2 SOL per 100 trades if hypothesis holds.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-20 — INVALID at n=100+: total<0, drop_top3<0. TP=75 + tighter SL=20 was the most aggressive of the v27 variants. The SL tightening hypothesis (-24% net realized loss instead of -36% per SL fire) didn't compensate for the TP=75 problem. SL=20 fired MORE often (more trades crossed -20% than -30%) AND each SL fire was on a trade that would have hit the wrong TP threshold anyway. Compound effect: more SL fires + same broken TP target = worst of the three v27 variants. Lesson on combined changes: when stacking two changes (TP shift + SL shift), if EITHER change is wrong-direction, the combination is worse than either alone. Test single-lever changes before stacking.

rows.hypothesis: v21-bottom-excluded variant: TP=100→75 + trailingTpEnabled=true + trailingTpDropPct=10. Tests whether the lower TP threshold (catching more wins per peak-analysis EV) combined with trailing TP (capturing right-tail upside past +75%) produces the best of both worlds. Mechanics: once price hits +75% the position flips to trailing mode, exits when price drops 10% from post-TP HWM. With the position-manager timeout fix (deployed 2026-05-17), trades in trailing mode can extend past the 300s maxHold up to 900s (3x cap). Direct test of whether 'lower TP + trailing' captures more value than the static-TP options. The TP=75 base means trailing activates 2x more often than it would at TP=100 — more chances to catch the right tail.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-20 — INVALID at n=100+: total<0, drop_top3<0. HIT-KILL. TP=75 + trailing TP=10% on the bot-excluded universe. The trailing TP modification did NOT rescue the TP=75 base — losses still accumulated. Confirms two patterns simultaneously: (1) TP=75 is wrong for this universe (per v27-bot-excl-tp75 result), AND (2) trailing TP doesn't compensate when the underlying TP threshold is too low. Mechanism: trailing TP only fires when the +75% threshold is crossed; if the +75% threshold itself is selecting for the wrong subset (brief spikes that retrace), trailing the exit doesn't help — the population was wrong from the start. Lesson: trailing TP layers on TOP of a TP threshold — it can't fix a bad threshold.

rows.hypothesis: vwap_pullback strict + climbing_t30_t45 shows opt_avg_ret=17.51% at TP150/SL30 in research (n=51, 74.5% WR). Closest analog strategies (v25-bot-excl-climbing) are STRENGTHENING. Propose shadow test at TP150/SL30, entry@T+60, to validate research vs live.

Mirror of v9shadow-velmono-dev (current #1 by net SOL: n=31, net +4.33, drop_top3 +2.67, mo +9.04 SOL/mo) with trailingTpEnabled=true and trailingTpDropPct=10. Hypothesis: the vel20-50+mono>0.5+dev>5% combo selects for tokens with strong path-shape signal — many of the TP-exits at +100% probably keep running. Trailing 10% from post-TP HWM should capture the right tail without giving back the whole pump. Requires the timeout fix (deployed alongside) so 300s maxHold doesn't force-exit trades still trailing.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-21 — n=9, net -0.52 SOL, drop_top3 -1.13 SOL. 1 trailing_tp exit fired across 9 trades. Avg net -11.48% vs base v9shadow-velmono-dev's +22.11% on the same filter universe. 33pp avg gap on small sample, but the drop_top3 is decisively negative. The 1 trailing exit didn't rescue the cohort.

rows.hypothesis: Mirror of v10-best-double (current #2 by net SOL: n=84, net +4.26, drop_top3 +1.94, mo +8.58 SOL/mo) with trailingTpEnabled=true / dropPct=10. v10-best-double uses TP=150/SL=25 on vel20-50+dd>-10%. The wider 150% TP already captures more of the right tail than 100% — adding trailing on top should compound. Tradeoff: with TP=150, trailing only activates on the +150% trades (the rarest), so the marginal impact is smaller than on TP=100 strategies.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-21 — n=22, net -0.76 SOL, drop_top3 -1.49 SOL. ZERO trailing_tp exits across all 22 trades — the TP=150 threshold was never reached. Strategy ran as 'trailing-TP-disabled but trailing-flag-enabled' which is functionally identical to base but with worse trade selection in the run window. Verdict on the trailing TP hypothesis for best-double universe: NULL — can't even test it because TP=150 is too far for this universe to hit. Lesson: trailing TP only matters if TP is reachable; for high-TP strategies the experiment requires lowering TP first.

rows.hypothesis: Mirror of v25-bot-excl-climbing (current #3 by net SOL, also TOP by monthly run rate at +36.75 SOL/mo: n=70, net +3.08, drop_top3 +1.44) with trailingTpEnabled=true / dropPct=10. The bottom-excluded + climbing_t30_t45 combo selects for tokens that have already shown confirmed upward path momentum by T+45 — these are exactly the tokens most likely to keep running past TP=100%. Trailing TP should have the largest expected lift on this strategy of all 5.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-21 — n=125 (largest v26 sample), net -0.02 SOL, drop_top3 -2.46 SOL. Trailing TP IS firing here (10 trailing_tp exits at 8% rate, similar to base's 10.3% take_profit rate). But the avg per trade is -3pp worse than base v25-bot-excl-climbing (+2.90%). Top winners DID capture moonshots (+205%, +158%, +125% — much higher than static TP=100), BUT near-miss trailing exits cluster around +85-95% net which is BELOW static TP. The arithmetic mean drops because near-misses outnumber moonshots on this universe. drop_top3 -2.46 confirms the wins are outlier-driven; strip the 3 moonshots and the strategy bleeds. The 'near-miss failure mode' confirmed on the largest sample we have.

rows.hypothesis: Mirror of v20-vel2050-dd-base (current #4 by net SOL: n=34, net +3.06, drop_top3 +0.80, mo +14.45 SOL/mo) with trailingTpEnabled=true / dropPct=10. Same underlying filter universe as v10-best-double but with entry-gate [5,100] instead of [-99,1000] and SL=30 instead of 25. Cleaner test of the trailing-TP effect since the entry-gated population is more focused on healthy starts.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-21 — n=17, net -0.85 SOL, drop_top3 -1.59 SOL. Same pattern as v26-best-double-ttp: ZERO trailing_tp exits across 17 trades. TP=150 too far for this universe to hit consistently. Median net -34.23% — the strategy is losing badly on the trades that DO fire. Confirms: trailing TP cannot rescue a universe that doesn't reach TP.

rows.hypothesis: Mirror of v9shadow-vel20-top5 (current #5 by net SOL: n=50, net +2.84, drop_top3 -0.21 [WARNING: lottery-ticket-driven], mo +5.29 SOL/mo) with trailingTpEnabled=true / dropPct=10. The drop_top3 is NEGATIVE on the base strategy — net is propped up by 3 outlier wins. The trailing-TP variant tests whether capturing more of the right tail on the existing winners produces a robust positive drop_top3 instead of relying on the same lottery tickets being even bigger. Higher kill bar reflects the questionable base-strategy edge.

vel<20 + top5<10% with TP150/SL30 should accumulate net SOL on live on-chain fills, validating the shadow result (n=47, +3.44 SOL, drop_top3=+0.39, mo=+7.39, edge-decay STABLE, Panel 11 STABLE on n=235). Orthogonal in candidate space to v9-velmono-dev-live-micro (different vel band — vel<20 vs vel>=20) so the two cannot fire on the same graduation. Thinner drop_top3 than v9-velmono (edge leans more on tail wins) and demanding TP=150% — main risks are PumpSwap 6004 (ExceededSlippage) on fast-movers and same 5s-polling discretization that affects all fixed-TP strategies.

updates.note: Tightened entry gate from [-99, +1000] to [+5, +100] to match the shadow mirror v9shadow-vel20-top5. Original wide gate was a copy-paste mistake on launch — the shadow's track record (n=47, +3.44 SOL net, drop_top3=+0.39) was measured under the [+5, +100] gate, and the live version was firing on candidates the shadow never validated (e.g. trade 10213 at pct_t30=-7%). Now the live and shadow universes match.

rows.hypothesis: Tests snipers <= 2 + liq > 100 — clean snipe environment combined with adequate T+30 pool depth. Hypothesis: low sniper count screens out the bot-rush rugs, and the liquidity floor ensures the entry/exit can actually fill.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-15 — INVALID at n=120: total_net_sol=-0.324, drop_top3=-3.295 SOL. The apparent +0.16 SOL net (Performance by Strategy panel) was driven by 3 lottery tickets — removing them flips the strategy to -3.3 SOL net. 3rd day on kill list. Lesson: the snipers<=2 + liq>100 combo doesn't actually produce edge after outlier-stripping; the sniper + liquidity signals together must NOT be additive on the win side.

rows.hypothesis: vel 20-50 + mono>0.5 + dev>5% with TP100/SL30 should accumulate net SOL on live on-chain fills, validating that the shadow result (n=30, +4.81 SOL, drop_top3=+3.16, mo=+10.61) survives real tx costs (Jito tip ~0.0001 SOL, ATA rent ~0.002 SOL one-time, RPC submission failures). Live test at 0.05 SOL/trade. Shadow already measures slippage on real pool state so the main delta vs shadow should be tx fees + occasional failed txs.

v21-bottom-excluded is the front-runner at n=128, net +4.10 SOL, but 44 SL fires (34%) are dragging the avg. Hypothesis: adding climbing_t30_t45 (recovery_t45_above_t30=1) at entry will pre-filter out the trades destined to hit SL — the C5 climbing gate excludes tokens whose price is dropping between t30 and t45, which is exactly the population that goes on to hit -30% SL. Trade-off: ~50% lower n, entry at T+60 instead of T+30. If the climbing filter cuts SL rate from 34% to ~18% while keeping TP rate similar, net SOL per trade should rise sharply.

v21-bottom-excluded variant: add sum_abs_returns_0_30 < 60 (realized vol proxy) to exclude whippy pre-entry tokens. Hypothesis: tokens with high pre-entry vol tend to whipsaw — the 30% SL is wide enough that the price spike upward looks like a winner, then a fast pullback hits the stop. Excluding whippy entries should preferentially cut SL count while preserving TP candidates. v17/v18 calm-survivor cohorts (sum_abs<60 + dd>-10) tested positive in early data. Different SL-reduction mechanism than the climbing variant: calmness is a population-shape filter (orthogonal to direction), climbing is a directional filter.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-15 — HIT-KILL: net -2.552 SOL, monthly -40.26 to -70.2 SOL/mo, drop_top3 -3.93. Extreme bleeder, no recovery path. Hypothesis falsified: the sum_abs<60 calmness filter on the bottom-excluded universe selects for the WORST subset, not the best. Calm pre-entry paths on the bottom-excluded population apparently correlate with subsequent dump (boredom signal) rather than steady appreciation. Lesson: sum_abs<60 is NOT universally protective — it depends on the underlying universe. Worked on v17/v18 (calm + dd>-10), failed catastrophically here.

rows.hypothesis: v21-bottom-excluded with breakevenStopPct=20 — same filters and TP/SL grid, but once a trade's HWM hits +20%, the SL line moves to 0%. Hypothesis: of the 44 current SL fires, some fraction touched +20% before bleeding back; those convert from -30% SL outcomes to 0% TO outcomes. Doesn't reduce SL COUNT but reduces SL MAGNITUDE on those trades. Cost: trades that briefly hit +20%, pull back, then would have recovered to a TP — get cut at 0% instead. If even 1/3 of current SL trades touched +20% first, this saves ~4 SOL of losses vs the baseline +4.10 SOL net.

updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-15 — HIT-KILL: net -1.185 SOL, monthly -18.70 to -25.3 SOL/mo, drop_top3 -2.57. The breakeven stop hypothesis (convert -30% SLs to 0% TOs by moving SL to 0 once HWM hits +20%) FAILED. Two possible mechanisms: (a) trades that touched +20% then pulled back to 0% were going to recover to TP — the breakeven cut them prematurely; (b) the +20% touch is the WHM peak, so by the time we get back to 0% the gap-penalty cost on a stop fill makes the realized exit worse than -30%/total_size_sol. The breakeven mechanic looks elegant but degraded the strategy materially vs the v21-bottom-excluded baseline (which is +4 SOL on similar n).

rows.hypothesis: v21-bottom-excluded with TP=150 (was 100) and SL=25 (was 30). Same filter universe. updates.note: KILLED 2026-05-18 — INVALID at n=111: total_net_sol=-0.68 SOL, drop_top3=-2.42 SOL. Two hard SOL-bar gates failed at promotion-bar n. Stacking explicit mono>0.5 on top shrinks n by ~50% while filtering out borderline-mono trades that were actually contributing positive expected value (the +0-30% peak cluster). FOURTH v25 variant to fail (after calm, breakeven, tp150) — only v25-bot-excl-climbing and v25-bot-excl-vel2050 survive. Pattern: this universe responds poorly to most overlay filters; pure exclusion (v21-bottom-excluded) and the specific C5 climbing overlay (v25-bot-excl-climbing) are the only configurations that work. Future iterations should focus on exit-side tuning (TP/SL) — see v27 cohort — rather than entry-side overlay stacking.
